name: Draugr
description: The Draugr, also known as a draug, draugur, or aptrgangr, is an undead creature from Norse mythology. These beings are animated corpses, often guarding their burial mounds and treasures within. Draugr are typically depicted as grotesque and powerful, possessing supernatural strength, the ability to change their size, and control over the weather. They are often associated with a foul stench and a terrifying presence. The draugr's origins often stem from individuals who were evil or greedy in life, cursed, or died violently. They are not mindless zombies but retain a semblance of their former personalities, twisted and corrupted by their undead state. The touch of a draugr is said to be deadly, and they are notoriously difficult to defeat, requiring specific methods and often the courage of a true hero. Their existence serves as a grim reminder of the consequences of a dishonorable life and the lingering power of the dead.
subtitle: Undead guardian of burial mounds in Norse mythology.
appearance: Draugr are depicted as bloated, decaying corpses with a dark complexion. They possess glowing eyes, often described as burning coals, and may retain remnants of their burial garments or armor. Their size can vary, sometimes towering over living humans. They often emanate a foul odor of decay and death.
habitat: Draugr are typically found in their burial mounds, guarding the treasures they were buried with. They can also be found near places associated with their death or former life, such as their homes or battlefields.
behavior: Draugr are fiercely territorial and protective of their treasures. They are known for their aggression and malevolence towards the living, often attacking anyone who trespasses upon their domain. They are cunning and possess a twisted intelligence, using their abilities to terrorize and kill. They resent the living and seek to inflict pain and suffering.
weaknesses: Specific Weapons (e.g., a sword from a specific lineage)

keyMyths.title: The Saga of Grettir the Strong
keyMyths.description: Grettir faces the draugr Glámr, a fearsome and powerful undead being. The battle is long and arduous, with Glámr proving a formidable opponent. Grettir ultimately defeats Glámr by decapitating him, but not before Glámr curses Grettir, leading to his eventual downfall. The story highlights the immense strength and danger of draugr.

keyMyths.title: The Story of Thorolf Foot-Bitter
keyMyths.description: Thorolf Foot-Bitter returns as a draugr after his death, terrorizing his family and community. He is eventually subdued by his son, Ketil Trout, who manages to bind the draugr and prevent him from causing further harm. This myth demonstrates the draugr's ability to haunt and harm their loved ones.

keyMyths.title: The Account of Asmund and Arinbjorn
keyMyths.description: Asmund and Arinbjorn, sworn blood brothers, make a pact to be buried together. When Asmund dies, Arinbjorn is buried alive with him. Asmund returns as a draugr, attacking and feeding on Arinbjorn. Arinbjorn eventually manages to kill Asmund and escape the burial mound. This highlights the brutal and horrifying nature of the draugr.

sources.author: Ellis Davidson, H.R.
sources.work: Gods and Myths of Northern Europe
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of Norse mythology, including descriptions of draugr and their role in various sagas.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive overview of Norse mythology, including descriptions of draugr and their role in various sagas.

sources.author: Turville-Petre, E.O.G.
sources.work: Myth and Religion of the North: The Religion of Ancient Scandinavia
sources.description: Offers an in-depth analysis of Norse religious beliefs and practices, shedding light on the cultural context of the draugr.
sources.text: Offers an in-depth analysis of Norse religious beliefs and practices, shedding light on the cultural context of the draugr.

sources.author: Byock, Jesse L.
sources.description: Explores the social and cultural aspects of Viking Age Iceland, where many of the sagas featuring draugr originated, providing context to the beliefs surrounding these creatures.
